name: design-and-implement-a-20260419-1105
idea: Design and implement a `moderation_decisions` table in the project's Supabase DB that tracks every time a Discord reaction is deleted/removed, with full details: reactor user_id/name, emoji, message_id/channel_id/guild_id, message author, message content snippet, removal timestamp, and a classification of WHO/WHAT removed it and WHY. Classifications should distinguish: (a) bot auto-removal for restricted emoji (flags/political/religious) via reactor_cog.py's restricted-emoji branch; (b) user self-removal (the reactor removed their own reaction); (c) other-user or moderator removal (someone else removed it, typically a mod); (d) bot "clear all" or message-delete cascades if distinguishable. Write paths to cover: (1) `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py` — the restricted-emoji auto-remove branch (must log BEFORE or alongside `message.remove_reaction` with classification='bot_auto_restricted' and reason=the specific category: flag/political/religious); (2) `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py` `on_raw_reaction_remove` — user-driven removals; compute classification by comparing `payload.user_id` to the reactor from the original add event (if available via discord_reaction_log). (3) Possibly the LoggerCog `_update_reaction` call path at `src/features/logging/logger_cog.py` if that's the right seam. Investigate the code first to pick the cleanest seam. Backfill consideration: there is an existing `discord_reaction_log` table that has `add` and `remove` events with emoji/user_id/message_id/guild_id/created_at — decide whether `moderation_decisions` is a new derived table or a view over that log, or a net-new write path with richer context that `discord_reaction_log` lacks. Bonus (include if cheap, skip if it expands scope): DM the ADMIN_USER_ID when a removal classified as 'suspicious' is logged — where suspicious = user removes their OWN prior 🤮 / 👎 / 😭 reaction (i.e. quietly retracting a negative reaction). Use the existing admin DM pattern from `src/common/base_bot.py:176-183`. Keep the implementation minimal and localized. Do NOT add feature flags, migrations framework, or new config surfaces beyond what's strictly required.
